name: Halfway to Hana + Waterfall & Garden of Eden Tour
description:
Halfway to Hana + Garden of Eden Tour
Itinerary:

Welcome aboard our guided tour as we embark on a thrilling journey along the winding road to Hana, one of the most scenic routes in all of Hawaii. Our itinerary for the day includes:

Departure from Kahului 8AM (checkin at 745AM): Our van is equipped with all the necessary safety features, and our experienced driver will make sure your ride is smooth and safe along the winding Road to Hana.

Ke'anae Peninsula: First we'll head to the Ke'anae Peninsula, a small village that offers a glimpse into old Hawaii. We'll stop at the iconic Ke'anae Peninsula Church, a picturesque church that has stood for over a century. You'll have the chance to admire the stunning architecture of this historic church, which is surrounded by lush greenery and overlooks the stunning blue waters of the Pacific Ocean.

Our exciting itinerary includes a delightful stop at a magnificent waterfall, where we can all take a dip and have a splashing good time. You'll have the chance to swim in the crystal-clear waters and embrace the beauty of nature.

Garden of Eden: With our guided tour, you can skip the long lines at The Garden of Eden and enter through a private entrance, complete with convenient private parking. This exclusive access ensures that you have more time to explore the beautif
highlights: Safe and reliable transportation to ensure a smooth ride along the winding road.
Lunch and snacks to keep you fueled and energized throughout the trip.
Ample water and other beverages to keep you well-hydrated during the journey.
An unforgettable experience that promises to be both enjoyable and fun.

name: West Maui Adventure (ʻIao Valley Option) (API Only)
description:
Iao Valley Option
DEPARTURE & DURATION

Flexible departure time between 8am -1pm
Recommend 8am-10am
Sunset Excursion – 1pm
7 hours

ABOUT
Experience the entire coast of Maui’s western volcano
Traverse an area rarely visited by tourists and void of tour busses
Take in the breathtaking views of rugged coastline, wind swept cliffs and magnificent valleys
Be a guest in the Hawaii of old while visiting Kahakuloa, an isolated Hawaiian settlement
Visit a cliff top gallery featuring over 100 local artisans
Examine the remote Bell Stone lava fields
Observe Oceanic Tidal Pools
Be impressed by the powerful blow hole at Nakalele Point
Taste local treats & sweets
Admire the vibrant coral reef at Honolulu Bay
'Iao Valley - drive into the sunken caldera of our western volcano
ʻIao Needle - View the 2,200-foot natural basalt tower
Wailuku River - Maui's largest stream includes a bounty of fresh flowing water
Kepaniwai Gardens - a cultural garden created in tribute to the ethnicities that arrived to pioneer the plantation
If it’s Humpback Whale season (December through May) stop to watch them spout, jump and play
BOOK NOW!
Cancellations

Enter a sea cave that exits at the Pacific Ocean

description:
Join us for a Romantic Sunset Cruise!
Rates & Duration

S/V Artemis ~ 47' Sailing Catamaran (Up to 6 passengers) - $2,400

About

Join us for your own Private Sunset sail. Perfect for celebrating birthdays, anniversaries, proposals, bachelorette parties, quality family time, and more!

Take your loved one and friends sailing on your own boat with the help of a few crew. If desired, we offer a hands-on learning experience for those who wish to learn about sailing! Unlike many other companies that motor the majority of the time, we pride ourselves on sailing as much as possible.

Want to spend a little extra time on the water? Add an extra hour on to your sail.

For 7-12 guests we offer the opportunity to charter the fleet for a buddy boat sail. We depart at the same time and set sail side-by-side for a once in a lifetime sunset adventure.

name: Dog Head Hook Blacksmith Experience : Intermediate Level
description:
4 Hours • Ages 10+ Limited to 4 participants

5 
stars
 

18 Google reviews

Discover the ancient art of blacksmithing in our immersive Blacksmith Experience Class
Duration

4 hours
limited to 4 participants, small class size

About

You'll learn the techniques of forging while crafting your very own "decorative dog head hook (for leashes!!)." Under the guidance of our skilled blacksmith, you'll learn several essential blacksmithing skills including: Forge set up, parts of the anvil, hammer technique, bending, folding, forging, hot cutting, fullering, punching, drawing, tapering, and rolling a fenial. When finished you’ll leave with a steel creation you hand forged, hand hammered into a unique piece of functional art. You literally punch in the facial expressions of your doggy!

This is an intermediate level class. It requires slightly above a beginner's baseline hammer skills. It is also more physical than the other project. Please be prepared to work hard in this class.

This class offers an unforgettable opportunity to delve into the fascinating world of metalwork. Book your spot now and embark on a journey of skill, craftsmanship, and creativity in the heart of paradise!

name: Private Charter
description:
6 Hours • Up to 12 People • Call to Book

4.9 
stars
 

411 Google reviews

Loading
Relax and Enjoy the Dive!
Duration

6 hours

About

Our boat is yours! On this private boat trip you get to experience the waters of Maui to yourself. We take you to enjoy two of our most popular dive/ snorkel sites but requests are always welcomed. This is a 5- 6 hour day with 2 locations slated. We provide drinks, light snacks, and fruit. Feel free to bring any other items you would like to enjoy. BYOB for adults (21 years or older) after the dives/ swim sessions are completed. The private charter is for a total of 12 people (potentially a few more if it is a snorkel only charter). Please feel free to call us with any special requests or questions.

Itinerary

Every day is different on Island Style Snorkeling. Our first stop is typically the beautiful Molokini Crater. We start nice and early to ensure we get a great spot at the Crater without a crowd. With typically over 100' of visibility and very calm waters, Molokini Crater showcases some of the best conditions Maui has to offer. Our guides will take you to the best spots that day and show you the beauty of the marine preserve. Our second stop is determined by conditions, passengers on board, and where our captain wants to adventure. It is typically another one of our beautiful South Shore dive and snorkel sites with the possibility to see turtles, wrecks, caverns, and/or anything else you may not have seen at Molokini and want to experie

name: Sunset Whale Watch and 1 Tank Night Dive
description:
Yes, you CAN have the best of both worlds! Check in at 4:PM for your sunset whale watch and follow it up with a 1 tank night dive!
Times

1 Hour Before Sunset, varies seasonally

About

Certified divers will enjoy this trip. Night Dives are suitable for experienced divers as well as novices and our crew does their best to group divers according to experience level with the goal of providing the best trip for each type of customer! IMPORTANT NOTE: If it has been over two years since your last dive, Maui Diamond require that you complete a Refresher Dive prior to your boat trip or hire a Private Dive Guide for your dives.

What to Expect:

We'll meet at the boat and 4:00 and head out for a couple of hours of whale watching until the sun goes down. After that, we'll tuck in along the Pali to an area of the coastline that is sheltered from the wind. All the cool critters come out at night right after the fish go to bed, so divers can expect to see invertebrates such as nudibranches, lobsters, octopus and squids.

Divers must provide their own lights

DIVER MEDICAL FORM REQUIRED: All certified divers are required to complete a medical history form and some will be required to obtain their physician's release to participate in scuba diving. Here's a link to the form for easy reference and in case it's needed for a doctor visit.

name: Maui Volcano Quest
description:
DEPARTURE & DURATION

Morning departure 8am
Duration is approximately 8 hours

About:
Start at sea level, then traverse through a variety of terrain including sugar cane fields, rolling countryside, green pastureland and old lava fields
Arrive at Haleakala Crater – approximately 10,000 feet above the ocean!
Peruse the Haleakala Visitor Center which showcases cultural & natural history exhibits, books and maps
Walk the Pa Ka’oao Trail (.4 mile round-trip, 20-minute trail) to observe ancient rock walls, shelters and an outstanding crater view
Drive to Pu’u’ula’ula where we explore the summit (15-minute walk) and examine the highly endangered, endemic Silversword plant
Gaze at the horizon, revealing the outer lying islands of Lana’i, Kaho’olawe and Hawai’i (Big Island)
Enjoy a light refreshment of fresh pineapple & banana bread
Take a stroll through a cloud forest featuring a hundred-year-old grove of cedar, pine, spruce & eucalyptus (.5 mile, 30-minute trail)
Study flora, fauna and the effects of invasive species
Watc

name: Pride of Maui 'Elua Luxury Molokini and Turtle Town Snorkel Tour
description:
About

The Pride of Maui ‘Elua is Maui’s first and only air-conditioned luxury yacht, offering private charters to Maui’s premiere snorkeling destinations. With over 2,000 SF of open deck space, she offers unobstructed panoramic views and photo opportunities.

Leaving from Ma'alaea Harbor in the morning, guests can look forward to arriving at Molokini when ocean conditions and water clarity is at its best for viewing Maui marine life. With an expert captain and crew, this 5 hour, limited capacity Molokini snorkel tour includes all snorkel gear and instruction, safety information, a delicious breakfast, BBQ lunch, and an open bar with local Maui beers, premium wines and Mai Tais.

On a Molokini snorkel tour, guests can expect to cruise the ocean in comfort on Maui’s first and only air-conditioned luxury yacht. With over 40 years of experience in Hawaiian waters, our tours are operated by seasoned crew with a passion for providing unsurpassed guest service and operating at a limited capacity to ensure that guests have the best experience possible.

What's included
Menu

name: The Road to Hana
description:
100 Ho'okele St, Kahului, HI 96732

For all ages! • Up to 5 People • Starting @ $1080

Free cancellation
Instant confirmation
Once in a lifetime photography expedition!
Duration
6 or 8 Hours
About
Travel the most breathtaking road in the world and gain priceless photography tips from master photographer Daniel Sullivan.
Join photographer Daniel Sullivan for a private custom designed photography tour. The landscape on Maui is extremely diverse and like nothing else on the planet. Black sand beaches, hidden waterfalls, bamboo forests, rainbow eucalyptuses and lost Hawaiian temples are a few of the landscapes you will explore.
You will photograph turtles on the beach, massive waterfalls, taro patches and vestiges of ancient Hawaii.
Experience a side of Maui few people get to see while learning to be a better photographer.
From the crashing waves of Jaws to the summit of Haleakala, capture stunning images in the field and hone your skills with master photographer Daniel Sullivan in this once in a lifetime photography expedition.
What to bring
Good walking shoes, Sunscreen, Hat, Bathing suit (optional)
Snacks and drinks provided
For a full day pack a lunch
Gear
Camera
Wide angle lens such as 14-24 or 24-70
Telephoto lens (70-200) optional
Filters 10 stop nd filter optional

name: Luxury Full-Circle Road to Hana Tour - Small Group
description:
Ride in Luxury on a Platinum Minibus

Built with a high roof so it’s tall enough to stand up and easy to get in and out at all of our stops. You’ll enjoy the comfort of air conditioning, high back leather seats, and the safety of 3 point safety seat belts and USB charging stations in every row to charge your phones. This vehicle is elevated so you ride higher than the guardrails and is designed with extra large windows which makes it easy to see everything around you. It's custom automatic European Portal Door and hardwood grain flooring add a special touch of luxury.

Times and Duration
Daily
Hotel pickups approximately 6-6:45AM
Return approximately 3:30-4:30PM
About

Long before the thought crossed our minds of doing a tour out to Hana, we drove this windy road of 600+ curves and 50+ one-lane bridges with our family in tow at least a gazillion times. Every time we travel the road we stand in awe of God’s fingerprints and the beauty that he created for us to enjoy. This famous highway of lush green valleys, bamboo forests, running streams and the smell of the sweet ginger lilies will fill your soul and spirit with gratitude of being alive and the privilege of witnessing such beauty.

As locals and native Hawaiians, we know that there’s more about this curvy road than its lush tropical beauty and we want you to learn about the history, the stories, and tales of this tropical rainforest that was once

name: Private Bottom Fishing Charter
description:
With over 30 years of exploring Maui waters, we take you straight to great bottom fishing grounds for a chance to hook in to a variety of fish. We are looking for rare, beautiful, feisty, big, personal bests, and if you'd like to keep your catch for dinner, tasty fish.
Times and Duration
Daily departures at 7:30 am
4-6+ Hour Private Charter
Up to 8 fishing and 2 riding
What's included
About

Bait, tackle, and the personal attention of our experienced captain and crew are all provided.

Marjorie Ann is a 36′ Chesapeake Bay deadrise style fishing boat with a comfortable cabin, large back deck, and plenty of shade. She was built in Maryland in 2010 and was purchased by Maui Fun Charters in late 2012 to be used for Maui style bottom fishing. She comes equipped with an on-board head, and the latest modern electronics and fishing tackle.

Our private charters offer an exclusive personalized experience. We cater to the weather, the bite, and your preference. The bottom fish bite is on… add an hour! The marlin bite is on… skip the bottom fishing and try your luck at reeling in a big one. We catch one that could be sent back down live to tempt a predatory fish… let's go!

name: Maui's Private Road To Hana Full Day Waterfall Joyride
description:
Set out on a full-day journey along Maui’s legendary Road to Hana in the comfort of a luxury Mercedes-Benz van. Let your expert local guide handle the winding roads as you relax and take in lush rainforests, cascading waterfalls, and dramatic ocean views. Throughout the day, enjoy handpicked stops at scenic lookouts, tropical gardens, and hidden waterfalls, where you can sample fresh island food and local treats.

Travel in style with round-trip hotel pickup, chilled beverages, and spacious seating for a stress-free adventure. Your guide brings the island to life with captivating stories, Hawaiian legends, and cultural insights that make every curve of the road meaningful. Experience the magic of Hana without the crowds or the driving, just pure Maui beauty, comfort, and discovery from start to finish.

name: Birdwatching Private Tour
description:
Explore Maui’s diverse birdlife on a guided seven-hour tour that visits the Haleakala summit, Hosmer’s Grove, Kealia National Wildlife Refuge, and scenic coastal areas. Search for endemic species such as pueo, nene, ʻApapane, ʻIʻiwi and ʻAmakihi, plus shorebirds, migrants and introduced species. The guide provides ecological, historical and cultural interpretation while you enjoy complementary juices, fruit, snacks and a full picnic lunch. Binoculars are available at no extra charge and participants can expect to see between 18 and 32 species on a typical day.

What's included

Guided birdwatching with interpretation of ecology, history and myths

Visits to Haleakala summit, Hosmer's Grove, Kealia National Wildlife Refuge, and coastal birding areas

Complimentary juices, fruits and snacks

Full picnic lunch

Binocular use available at no extra charge

A checklist to track species observed

Pick up or meet at Maui Tropical Plantation at 8:00 AM.
Drive to Haleakala summit to search for pueo, nene and chukar and enjoy summit views.
Visit Hosmer's Grove to look for ʻApapane, ʻIʻiwi, ʻAmakihi and other native forest birds.
Picnic lunch in Haleakala Nati

name: Haleakala Hiking
description:
Put on your boots and explore Haleakala National Park on a guided full-day hiking tour that travels through multiple climate zones to dramatic crater viewpoints. Choose from moderate summit hikes such as Sliding Sands and Halemau’u, or lower-elevation nature walks at Hosmer’s Grove. Along the way your guide shares Maui’s geology, history, and cultural stories while you look for endemic birds and take in sweeping vistas. The tour includes snacks, hot and cold beverages, water, and a picnic lunch.

Hike the Sliding Sands trail from the summit (approx. 2 miles roundtrip), adjustable by ability.
Hike the Halemau’u trail (approx. 2.2 miles roundtrip) with native shrub and bird viewing and northern rim viewpoints.
Optional lower-elevation nature walk at Hosmer’s Grove as an easier alternative.

description: We believe the more knowledge you have about the ocean the better surfer you will be. We have method of teaching that helps individuals learn crucial information enabling you to understand all the different aspects of surfing.

name: Kayak, Snorkel & Whale Watch with Photo and Video
description:
Paddle along Maui’s coastline on a guided kayak experience before snorkeling at Turtle Town. Small group, fully guided, high end gear, and designed for a immersive ocean experience. Swim with sea turtles, explore the reef, and during whale season— watch playful humpback whales from the comfort of your kayak.

What's included

Top-tier snorkel gear

Hand-picked engaging guides

High end Ocean Kayak + Paddles

Itinerary

Say Aloha— Check-In & Gear Setup (15 min)
Meet at the shoreline, get fitted with kayak and snorkel gear, and go over a short demonstration and safety briefing.

Kayak Launch (15 min)
Launch from Olowalu, the largest reef system on Maui and paddle in the shallow reefs with stunning panoramic views of the West Maui Mountains. The shallow reef system creates an opportunity to observe marine life directly from your kayak.

Whale Watching (Seasonal) (45 min) 
Observe the playful habits of one of the largest animals in the world, The Humpback Whale from your kayak.

Snorkel at Turtle Town (45 min) 
Anchor the kayaks and snorkel with your guide. Look for sea turtles, tropical fish, and diverse marine life at Maui’s largest reef, the Mother Reef. Your guide may also safely point out and share
highlights: Top-tier snorkel gear

name: Road Bike Tour
description: Ride alongside one of our experienced road cycling tour guides - and/or hire a sag vehicle - and experience the world-class roads of Maui like a local cyclist. Start and end your ride right at one of our bike shops.

name: Haleakala Downhill Upcountry Bike Tour
description: *Safety: You are riding on country roads and sharing the road with cars. There are switchbacks and turns that you need to navigate turns safely and stay on the right side of the lane/shoulder. This ride is 99.

name: Waterfall & Rainforest Hiking Adventure • Private Group
description: This is our most popular hike in an amazingly unique valley. Dazzling waterfalls from 10 to 40 feet high, large clear swimming pools under each of the falls, magical rainforests, and freshwater streams.

name: Waterfall & Rainforest Hiking Adventure • Meet in Central Maui
description: This is our most popular hike in an amazingly unique valley. Dazzling waterfalls from 10 to 40 feet high, large clear swimming pools under each of the falls, magical rainforests, and freshwater streams.

description: Experience the thrill of watching dolphins in the wild on our state-of-the-art luxury Schooner Creek sailing vessels.  This one-of-a-kind adventure is 2.5 hours and includes an island style lunch and refreshments.

name: Sunset Glow Snorkel @ Turtle Town With Photo and Video
description: Being the only ones on Maui to offer a sunset glow tour sets this entire activity apart! Sunset into dusk is an amazing time to snorkel because of the variety of marine life you will see, that isn't out during the day.

name: Maui's Sea Turtles & Waterfalls: Taste of Hana Guided Tour
description: We do require a minimum of 4 guests for our trip to be confirmed. If your booking doesn’t meet that minimum right away, don’t worry we’ll always reach out ahead of time to let you know the status and do our best to get you on a confirmed trip that fi...

name: MAKALI'I-THE RISING STAR OF THE PACIFIC
description:
Authentic Performances from Tahiti, Hawai‘i, Aotearoa (Māori), and Samoa.
Immersive Lighting simulating fire and water for a modern, cinematic twist.
High-Energy Dance & Chant, including Haka, Hula Kahiko, Hula ‘Auana, and more.

name: Kids Surf Camp
description: Our experienced instructors will give your Kids their personal attention and share the surfing experience with them. Learning to surf should be safe and fun, and  empowering. Kids need closer supervision than adults.

name: Makena Turtle Town Eco Adventure
description: This eco adventure is guided and include 2 snorkel sights, kayak gear, life jackets, snorkel gear, a bottle of water and a light snack. Typically, group sizes are held to 10 people or less to ensure a quality and personalized experience.

name: Tropical Fruit Tasting Walking Tour
description: After the tasting, our knowledgeable guides will take you on a stroll. You will get a closer look at the plants producing this excellent fruit.

name: Unique Road to Hana
description:
We pick you up from your hotel or meet in Kahului at 8am—but it’s a private tour, so it’s flexible if you need it to be, just let us know!
Meet at Target Kahului, or free hotel pick-up for South side properties (Kihei, Wailea, Ma'alaea).

description:
Integrated Organic Farming Tour
Our kid and family friendly adventure tour takes place on the grounds of the Maui Dragonfruit Farm, a USDA certified organic farm with incredible ocean and West Maui Mountain views.

description: Our educational, hands on butterfly farm tours are now available and take place ​at The Maui Butt​erfly Farm at 820 Olowalu Village Rd, Lahaina, Hi 96761

name: Open Water Scuba Diver Referral Course
description: All of our courses are taught in small groups - so that you get the most out of your time underwater. This means you'll receive specialized attention, feel safer, and get to spend more time underwater.

name: Whale Watch and/or Scenic Wildlife Tour
description: Or if whales season has ended, cruise along the West Maui Coast with our peddle drive system, freeing your hands for a cold beverage, GoPro or even long-range camera lens for those beautiful West Maui Mountain photos.

name: Group Bottom Fishing Tour
description: Although rare, we reserve the right to change or cancel tours based on unsafe weather or ocean conditions. If we are unable to find another availability, a full refund is provided.

description: This course is designed as a direct follow-up to the PADI Open Water certification program. Advanced Open Water Certification builds on what you've learned previously, introducing new activities and new ways to have fun scuba diving.

name: Waterfall & Rainforest Hiking Adventure • South Maui Hotel Transfers

description: Tucked beneath the cliffs are large coral outcroppings that make for beautiful underwater topography and snorkeling. We have a variety of sites along this coast which are some of Maui’s premier snorkel sites including coral gardens and Olowalu.

name: Off Road Coastal Tour In Bronco With Snorkel Photo and Video
description: Come cruise with us in this semi-private off road + Snorkel adventure! During our 3-4 hours together we will load your crew into our premium 2024 4x4 off road vehicle that comes fully loaded with aftermarket audio system, and 35" off road tires.

name: Ultimate Maui Astrophotography
description:
This private workshop is designed for guests that want to spend more time under the stars regardless if you are a beginner or are more advanced in your astrophotography journey. Let's explore the night sky with several different locations with compositions pending your desires.

What's included

Personalized private workshop under the night sky (in the field) based on where you are at in your photography journey

Shoot test shots at different apertures, shutter speeds, ISO and discuss how each of these will affect you image

Step by step discussion, details, and other helpful tips and suggestions

Various scenarios for adjusting settings and explanation of each

Roughly 3 - 3.5 hours of guidance. Please note that this will include time to arrive and walk any trails

Pre-arrival phone call before you arrive to the island to discuss any gear you may wish to bring

Please note that taking images of the galactic core may not be visible during certain times of the year and is not guaranteed.

Stay connected with me, even after you leave the island. I always love hearing back from past students!

What's not included

Guests will need to provide their own gear for this experience. We highly suggest bringing a waterproof backpack and a jacket in the event of a passing shower. St

name: Private Maui’s Sea Turtles, Waterfalls and Taste of Hana Adventure
description:
Say goodbye to rigid schedules and book this half-day Road to Hana day trip that gives you the flexibility to customize the itinerary.

The Hana Highway weaves along the northeast coast of Maui, and it’s well known on the island for the beaches, waterfalls, and rainforest beauty.

You will spend your day exploring volcanic beaches, soaking up the beautiful landscapes, and learning about Hawaiian culture. Highlights include visiting without having to focus on driving, with this guide-led tour.

What's included

Your trip includes ice cold beverages and water, round-trip hotel transport and a certified guide.

What's not included

Gratuities
Change of clothes (If you swim)
Reef Shoes (for waterfall)
Small Jacket or rain coat

Itinerary

These are our main stops. Please let us know if you have any special requests. Pua'a Ka'a Waterfall is our turnaround point.

Hana Highway
Road to Hana It's all about the journey on the Road to Hana, traveling in style and comfort along cascading waterfalls, scenic, lush vistas, and windy roads. You'll return along the same coastal drive on reverse, but with a different perspective.

Paia (Pass By)
Drive-by Pa'ia town with its rich history of plantation camps, w
highlights:

Drive-by Pa'ia town with its rich history of plantation camps, which housed workers of the old Paia Sugar Mill.

description: As you traverse the lush, verdant trails, your native guide — deeply rooted in the local community — will share tales and traditions of the Hawaiian people, offering a rare and personal perspective of Maui’s heritage.

description: Late risers, sleep in, then join us for an afternoon of exceptional snorkeling. We routinely snorkel with the turtles at Turtle Town or enjoy a variety of colorful marine animals at Olowalu or Coral Gardens, depending upon which location is the calme...

description: Ready to tie the knot? Join us on the beach in Maui, barefoot in the sand for your private elopement. Stress-free, fun and intimate. Limited to small groups and one location. 60 minutes of shooting & 75+ images edited in high resolution.

name: Maui's Sea Turtles & Waterfalls: Full-Day Road to Hana Guided Tour with Lunch Included
description:
Set out on a full-day journey along Maui’s legendary Road to Hana in the comfort of a luxury Mercedes-Benz van. Let your expert local guide handle the winding roads as you relax and take in lush rainforests, cascading waterfalls, and dramatic ocean views. Throughout the day, enjoy handpicked stops at scenic lookouts, tropical gardens, and hidden waterfalls, where you can sample fresh island food and local treats.

Included lunch at Hāna Ranch Restaurant. In the heart of Hāna, serving fresh farm-to-table dishes that highlight Maui’s local produce and proteins. Set at the Hāna-Maui Resort, it’s the perfect stop after the Waiʻānapanapa State Park with its warm island hospitality, beautiful upcountry-meets-coastal vibes, and a menu of comforting Hawaiian-inspired favorites done with care.

Travel in style with round-trip hotel pickup, chilled beverages, and spacious seating for a stress-free adventure. Your guide brings the island to life with captivating stories, Hawaiian legends, and cultural insights that make every curve of the road meaningful. Experience the magic of Hana without the crowds or the driving, just pure Maui beauty, comfort,

name: Premier Whale Watch *Private Charter* 3 HOURS + SNORKEL
description:
Personalized Experience with Maui's Whales

Experience Maui’s humpback whales on a fully private whale watch designed to give you the best memories for you and your friends and family. Aboard our fast, stable rigid-hulled raft, you’ll enjoy close-to-the-water viewing, expert interpretation of whale behavior, and the freedom to move, photograph, and observe without sharing space with other guests.
Enjoy additional time on the water observing the whales, OR jump in at one of Maui's premier snorkel sites to explore our underwater habitat and HEAR the whalesong as you float in the water.

Every tour is different. You may encounter mothers and calves, competitive pods, breaching whales, or hear haunting whale songs through our onboard hydrophone. Your experienced captain customizes each outing based on ocean conditions, whale activity, and your interests—whether you’re seeking a peaceful morning on calm seas or a more adventurous afternoon ride. description: Just a short distance off Maui’s shore, under the stunning Pacific blue water, lies a slice of history. Join us for a 2 tank guided boat to dive the Carthaginian wreck and a second breathtaking dive with turtles and sharks.

name: Private Sunset Charter • Monohull Nova
description:
Join us for a Romantic Sunset Cruise!
Rates & Duration

S/V Nova ~ 42' Sailing Monohull (Up to 6 passengers) - $1,440
Charter the Fleet (Up to 12 passengers) - $3,200

About

Join us for your own Private Sunset sail. Perfect for celebrating birthdays, anniversaries, proposals, bachelorette parties, quality family time, and more!

Take your loved one and friends sailing on your own boat with the help of a few crew. If desired, we offer a hands-on learning experience for those who wish to learn about sailing! Unlike many other companies that motor the majority of the time, we pride ourselves on sailing as much as possible.

Want to spend a little extra time on the water? Add an extra hour on to your sail.

For 7-12 guests we offer the opportunity to charter the fleet for a buddy boat sail. We depart at the same time and set sail side-by-side for a once in a lifetime sunset adventure.

What's Included

Light appetizers enjoyed onboard
Non-alcoholic beverages: water, juices, and sodas are provided

What's Not Included

Alcoholic beverages are not included. However, you are welcome to bring alcohol onboard and we have all the appropriate glassware for serving.

Gratuity is not included in your charter price
(Private yacht standard generally starts at 20%)

Cancellations

description:
Upgrade your Christmas Dinner Cruise to premium seating and secure a guaranteed private table for your party to enjoy a holiday feast in one of the world’s most beautiful settings.*

Our relaxing Christmas Dinner Cruise is a uniquely Maui experience, offering a delicious buffet of traditional favorites with all the fixings and some island-style influences. It’s a special way to celebrate the holiday, with spectacular sunset views of the Pacific Ocean, West Maui mountains, and neighbor islands. Likely appearances by humpback whales or dolphins make this a merry Christmas indeed.

Departs from conveniently located Ma'alaea Harbor; an easy drive from Kihei, Wailea, Makena, Paia, Upcountry, Wailuku and Kahului. Book your ticket now – New Year’s Eve trips sell out early.

name: Sunset Cocktail Cruise • Ma'alaea
description:
Enjoy sunset while enjoying tantalizing pupus (appetizers) and refreshing cocktails.

Our award-winning Sunset Cocktail Cruise is also a whalewatch ecotour during whale season, led by certified Marine Naturalists for fun and informative interpretation of humpback whale behaviors and characteristics. Watch mothers and calves interact, see males compete for females, and hear underwater whale songs. This unique excursion also includes an array of appetizers and drinks, plus an amazing Maui sunset.

Whale sightings are guaranteed or go again free on another whalewatch of equal or lesser value within one year. We are proud of our 97% sighting success rate.

name: Bamboo Forest Trek (API Only)
description:
Most Popular!!
DEPARTURE & DURATION

Morning departure between 7:45am
Duration is approximately 11 hours

About:
Experience the road less traveled
Traverse through verdant countryside, jagged lava fields and expansive pasture
View towering cliffs, bluffs and rugged coastline
Arrive at Haleakala National Park & The Pools of ‘Oheʻo
Take the Kuloa Point Trail to witness crashing waves at the mouth of the stream
Embark on a 4-mile hike through the famed Bamboo Forest
Marvel at a 200 foot and 400 foot waterfall
Admire an immense Banyan tree
Learn about indigenous plants and non-native species
Taste local, seasonal, exotic fruit at the Ono Farms Produce Stand
Arrive in Hāna town! The seat of power for all of Maui in the 15th Century and was once favored by Ali’i (Hawaiian royalty)
Explore Wai’anapanapa State Park
Touch a real black sand beach! (swimming optional)
Take a walk to a blowhole amongst a rugged coastline
Enter a sea cave that exits at the Pacific Ocean
Stop at Coconut Glen’s to enjoy culinary treats of ice cream and candy
Set out on the Road to Hāna – a scenic coastal drive
Witness many more waterfalls, shoreline, rainforest and flora & fauna
BOOK NOW
Cancellations

name: Haleakala Summit & Stroll (API Only)
description:
DEPARTURE & DURATION

Morning departure 7:45am
Sunset departure between 1:30pm & 2:30pm (depends on current sunset time)
Duration is approximately 5 hours

About:
Start at sea level, then traverse through a variety of terrain including sugar cane fields, rolling countryside, green pastureland and old lava fields
Take a stroll through a cloud forest featuring a hundred year old grove cedar, pine, spruce & eucalyptus (.5 mile, 30-minute trail)
Study flora, fauna and the effects of invasive species
Watch for native birds and listen to their calls
Enjoy a light refreshment of fresh pineapple & banana bread
Arrive at Haleakala Crater – approximately 10,000 feet above the ocean!
Peruse the Haleakala Visitor Center which showcases cultural & natural history exhibits, books and maps
Walk the Pa Ka’oao Trail (.4 mile round-trip, 20-minute trail) to observe ancient rock walls, shelters and an outstanding crater view
Drive to Pu’u’ula’ula where we explore the summit (15-minute walk) and examine the highly endangered, endemic Silversword plant
Gaze at the horizon, revealing the outer lying islands of Lana’i, Kaho’olawe and Hawai’i (Big Island)
Perch for sunset high above the coast and clouds
Return to our starting point
BOOK NOW
Cancellations

name: 2-Tank Local Morning Dive Charter
description:
Cruise along Kona's rugged coast in search of clear, calm, warm waters. Explore Kona's colorful reefs, swim through lava tunnels and keep an eye out for large marine life.

During your two dives, you'll have the opportunity to see fish, eels, octopus, and much much more as your divemaster guides you through Kona's reefs.

What's included

Divemaster

Snacks & water are provided during this trip

Tanks & Weights

Free Nitrox

What's not included

Dive Equipment (available for rent)

Towel

Reef Safe Suncreen

Itinerary
Meet at the boat where the crew will assemble your gear
Depart from Honokohau Harbor riding high above the sea surface in search of marine life
Dive briefing covering the first dive site
Gear up and hop in for the first guided dive. Dive the air in your tank and surface when you're ready to
Have snacks and de-brief as the boat heads to dive site #2 and the crew switches your tank to a fresh one
Site briefing #2 with your divemaster
Gear up and hop in for the second guided dive
Surface near the boat when you're ready and kick back in the warm sun as the crew breaks down your gear
Ride on the bow or upper deck as the Honu One returns to the harbor
Highlights
Colorful reef life & unique large animal encounters
Underwate

Your captain picks the day’s ultimate spot based on conditions, guest vibe, and where the ocean is showing off the most – guaranteeing you the best possible adventur

Durations

We find we can deliver the best experience by picking which sites we visit once we are out of the harbor. Kohala Coast water conditions are ever-changing, and the crew will make their site choice by best visibility, calmest waters and variety for our repeat dive guests. All the sites we visit have something great to offer every experience level and snorkelers as well.

Join us for a luxurious valet dive charter where we take care the details of your dive day for you. Our dive guides like to take it slow underwater so that you have a relaxing time and can enjoy the scenery while also being able to find the most creatures big and small

Snorkelers (8 years old and up) are welcome. Snorkelers are often entertained by passing dolphins or great whale activity.

Traveling with uncertified divers? They can book the 1 tank discover scuba course or open water certification course and join you on this charter.

3 trips or more gets you 10% off all dives.

name: Whale Watch Adventure
description:
Come join us for yet another spectacular adventure.

From December to April, the Humpback Whales come to give birth, breed and nurse their young.

Our tour offers you a rare opportunity to view, listen (we have hydrophones on board) and experience Humpback Whales. The Kona Ocean Adventures team will astound you with little-known facts local knowledge about these gentle giants as they play, court and teach their young….the longest known migration of any mammal…

The Humpback whales seen here are returning from their feeding grounds in Southeastern Alaska. They come here not to escape the cold but to give birth to their young. The reason is when a calf is born, it has no blubber insulation to keep it warm in the cold waters of Alaska. They seek out breeding waters here which offer temperatures of approximately 75 degrees.

What's included

2.5 hour whale watching adventure

Cancellations

Details
It’s an entirely different world when the sun sets below the horizon! Come explore how our ocean life transforms under when the moonlight!
About

Dive into a world of wonder with a guided night dive along Hilo’s coastline. As darkness falls, the reef comes alive with eels, nudibranchs, brittlestars, fireworms, and a plethora of nocturnal reef fish!

Your experienced dive master will guide you safely through this hidden underwater world, revealing creatures most divers never see. Leave your gear at home and dive in our top of the line equipment including 2018 Atomic Z3 regulators and Oceanic BCD's.

Experience the thrill of Hilo’s marine life under the moonlight—an unforgettable adventure for any underwater explorer.
Book your night dive and see the reef like never before!

name: Maunakea Stellar Explorer Hilo
description:
For an out-of-this-world experience, check out Maunakea Stellar Explorer from KapohoKine Adventures.
Duration

7 Hours

About

For an out-of-this-world experience, check out Maunakea Stellar Explorer from KapohoKine Adventures. This thrilling adventure is the only Big Island star tour to feature nighttime astronomical observing AND safe daytime solar viewing with all ages welcome! Our in-house stellar explorers are the best and most experienced in Hawaii with one having created the Mauna Kea Visitor’s Center and operated it for a decade. Others on staff have written noted articles and reviews for well-known astronomy magazines and have worked at world-class observatories. Start your adventure in quaint Hilo town where you’ll drive along the historic waterfront before arriving at Rainbow Falls. This iconic waterfall right off a postcard features an 80 foot tall twin waterfall system. Look for the rainbow effect that gives the waterfall its name! Continue traveling up the Wailuku River to Boiling Pots. Impressive photo opportunities are often found here when the river “boils” through depressions in the rocks. Explore Kaumana Caves, part of the lava tube system that brought lava 1.5 miles from Hilo-town in 1881.

name: Kona Haunted Hele 👻
description:
Our most popular tour!
Duration

2 Hours

TOUR OVERVIEW:
Step into the spooky past and supernatural happenings of Kailua-Kona on this evening tour. After the sun dips down, follow an expert local guide as you explore eerie tales of hauntings and real-life historical events that are sure to send chills down your spine.

​Meet up with your guide and set out on foot down the historic Ali'i Drive beginning the tour with a prayer of protection, a statement of intention, and a brief overview of Kona's fascinating, mystery-filled history. From there, make your way to various locations where supernatural events are believed to have occurred, from the historic location of human sacrifices to the path where the ghostly, torch-wielding Night Marchers are believed to walk.

Pass by the one-time royal summer palace of Hawaii's kings and queens, see the first Christian church in the islands, and roam among the tombstones in 2 cemeteries. Hear about the history of the old Kona Inn, built on the site of an ancient temple known to have more than 40 totem-style ki'i statues. At each location, put specialized paranormal activity detection equipment to the test as you try to seek out the energy of spiritual beings, and wrap up your haunted foray with a cleansing white sage smudge.
​

description:
1717 Kamehameha Ave Hilo, HI 96720

4, 6, or 8 hour charters • Ages 12+ • Up to 6 people

4.7 
stars
 

833 Google reviews

The Big Island of Hawaiʻi is one of the world’s top deep sea fishing destinations
Ages

Ages 12 and up. Minors under 18 must be accompanied by an adult.

About the Charter

Experience the thrill of deep sea fishing off the coast of Hilo on a private big game fishing charter. Step aboard our fully equipped sport fishing vessel and head out beyond the bay with our experienced local captain and CPR-trained crew. The waters off East Hawaiʻi are home to a wide variety of prized game fish, including Mahi Mahi, Ono, and Ahi tuna, offering anglers of all levels an unforgettable challenge.

As we cruise along Hilo’s stunning coastline, you’ll feel the warm trade winds and take in breathtaking views of Mauna Kea and the lush Hamakua cliffs. Our crew will guide you through the techniques and equipment used to target pelagic species, from setting the lines to reeling in your catch. Whether you’re a seasoned angler or a first-timer, this private charter offers a personalized, hands-on fishing experience you’ll never forget.

Light snacks, cold beverages, and all fishing gear are included. Guests are encouraged to bring a sack lunch and plenty of enthusiasm for a day out on the water.

What Makes This Experience Special
Private charter with expert captain and CPR-trained crew
Target big game species like Mahi Mahi, Ono, and Ahi tuna
All equipment provided: rods, reels

name: Waimea to Waipio Valley
description:
Experience the beauty of Hawaiʻi’s Hamakua Coast on a guided bike tour to Waipiʻo Valley, one of the most iconic and sacred landscapes on the Big Island. This scenic ride follows a quiet coastal road known for its lush rainforest, sweeping ocean views, and dramatic sea cliffs.
Our Hamakua Coast bike tour takes you through rolling terrain with constant views of the Pacific, hidden waterfalls, and deep green valleys carved by centuries of rainfall. Riding a premium bike (or e-bike), you’ll enjoy a comfortable, relaxed pace that lets you fully take in the scenery without feeling rushed.
The highlight of the tour is the Waipiʻo Valley lookout, where you’ll stop to view the valley far below — a stunning panorama of taro fields, a winding river, black sand beach, and towering cliffs often wrapped in mist. Your local guide shares stories about the area’s cultural significance, history, and natural features, adding depth and context to the ride.
This small-group Big Island bike tour is perfect for travelers looking for an active but approachable way to explore Hawaiʻi’s north shore. With limited group sizes, personalized guidance, and plenty of photo stops, this ride

Try SCUBA Experience
Ages

10 and up (minors under 18 must be accompanied by an adult)

About the Experience

Curious about scuba but not ready for the full ocean dive? Our Try SCUBA Experience is the perfect way to see if diving is right for you. This session includes the classroom and pool portions of our Discover Scuba Diving program and gives you a safe, affordable introduction to the world of underwater adventure.

The experience begins with a relaxed classroom orientation where your instructor will explain the basics of scuba equipment, breathing techniques, and safety. After that, you will jump into our heated indoor pool to learn the foundational skills of scuba in a calm, shallow environment. You will practice breathing underwater, moving weightlessly, and becoming comfortable with the gear at your own pace.

Whether you are looking for a unique date night, a family activity, or a fun group outing, this guided pool session lets you experience the excitement of scuba without needing to commit to the full ocean dive. It is all the fun of trying something new with none of the pressure.

What Makes This Experience Special
Perfect for anyone who wants to try scuba before signing up for the full course
Safe, controlled pool setting ideal for beginners
Learn the core skills from our Discover Scuba Diving program
Highly trained instructors who keep the experience relaxed and enjoyable

name: Evening Volcano Explorer - Kona Pick-Up
description:
Travel south into Kona coffee country for the first stop at Bay View Farm, a working 24 acre coffee farm nestled on the slopes of Mauna Loa Volcano just above beautiful Kealakekua Bay and known for the best Kona coffee since 1984.
Duration

12 Hours

About

Travel south into Kona coffee country for the first stop at Bay View Farm, a working 24 acre coffee farm nestled on the slopes of Mauna Loa Volcano just above beautiful Kealakekua Bay and known for the best Kona coffee since 1984. Here you’ll see coffee growing on the trees and learn about the various steps and processes in making coffee, all of which happen right here on the farm. You’ll sample 100% Kona Coffee at a gorgeous overlook with stunning views of Kealakekua Bay and the monument to Captain Cook standing on the only sliver of British land in the United States. The adventure continues as you head through the South Kona and Ka’u Districts. Stunning views, alternating between stark lava flows and Hawaiian dry forest, are visible through the huge picture windows of your tour vehicle. We stop briefly at Punalu’u Bake Shop, the southern most bakery in America. Offering the finest time-honored Hawaiian sweetbreads since 19

name: 1-Tank Pelagic Black Water Night Dive
description:
For the true adventurer.
Duration

2.5 Hours

About

The Pelagic Night Dive is a 1-tank black water dive off of the Kona coast. We start our Pelagic Night Dive 2-3 miles off the Kona coat. Then, floating tethered offshore in the deep dark sea, we wait to see what goes by, usually staying around 50 feet in depth. Our lights attract microscopic zooplankton like moths to a porch light, which in turn draws in miniature open ocean predators like jellyfish and squid. Hang suspended in the blackness of the Pacific Ocean as you experience what only a few ever dream of seeing. The Pelagic Night Dive, an experience of a lifetime! 

Dive Requirements:

This dive is for the confident diver that has excellent buoyancy skills and is self-reliant.

This is an advance dive.

You must have at least 50 logged dives to join us on this adventurous dive.

You must have been diving within the last 6 months.

You must be in good health and have NO heart conditions.

You must be able to converse in English or request a dive translator (additional fee applies).

Times:

Available Sunday and Wednesday.
We require a minimum of 4 guests to take you on this amazing dive adventure.

Check in:

Tour check-in location is at the Honokohau Small Boat Harbor.

Start times begin at 8:1

name: Manta Ray Late Night Snorkel in Keauhou - Kona
description:
Swim with the Majestic Manta Rays – A Must-Do on the Big Island

Kailua-Kona is one of the only places in the world where you can snorkel with manta rays at night—a rare and unforgettable experience.

At Hawaii Island & Ocean Tours, our mission is to get you as close to these incredible animals as safely and respectfully as possible. You'll float on the surface using our custom-built light boards, which attract zooplankton—drawing manta rays in for an up-close encounter.

Guests will swim approximately 50 feet from the boat to our paddleboard-based lighting system, which acts as a gathering point for the manta rays. To help you stay in the correct viewing position, you’ll wear a flotation belt secured around your ankles, keeping your body flat on the surface as you watch the manta rays feed and barrel roll beneath you.

⚠️ Important: This is an active ocean tour. Guests must know how to swim confidently, and this experience is not suitable for first-time snorkelers unless they are strong, independent swimmers. You must be comfortable in the ocean and able to follow guide instructions throughout the experience.

Our professional guides will assist you in positioning and safety, ensuring a once-in-a-lifetime chance to witness these gentle giants in their natural

description:
Travel south into Kona coffee country for the first stop at Bay View Farm, a working 24 acre coffee farm nestled on the slopes of Mauna Loa Volcano just above beautiful Kealakekua Bay and known for the best Kona coffee since 1984.
Duration

12 Hours

About

Travel south into Kona coffee country for the first stop at Bay View Farm, a working 24 acre coffee farm nestled on the slopes of Mauna Loa Volcano just above beautiful Kealakekua Bay and known for the best Kona coffee since 1984. Here you’ll see coffee growing on the trees and learn about the various steps and processes in making coffee, all of which happen right here on the farm. You’ll sample 100% Kona Coffee at a gorgeous overlook with stunning views of Kealakekua Bay and the monument to Captain Cook standing on the only sliver of British land in the United States. The adventure continues as you head through the South Kona and Ka’u Districts. Stunning views, alternating between stark lava flows and Hawaiian dry forest, are visible through the huge picture windows of your tour vehicle. We stop briefly at Punalu’u Bake Shop, the southern most bakery in America. Offering the finest time-honored Hawaiian sweetbread

name: Night Manta Experience
description: Go home and tell your family and friends that you did something they’ll never believe- you participated in a feeding frenzy in the warm waters of the Pacific Ocean in the darkness of night with creatures as large as 16ft across and weighing 1000lbs.

name: Manta Magic
description:
Manta Magic is a nighttime snorkeling experience off Kona that brings you face to face with graceful manta rays attracted to plankton around illuminated boards. Led by a swim guide, guests float at the surface and watch the manta rays swoop and feed in a spectacular, close-up ballet beneath the stars. The operator is Green Listed by the Manta Ray Advocates of Hawaii and provides equipment and nonalcoholic beverages to make this unforgettable marine encounter safe and comfortable.

What's included

Wetsuit top (optional)

Nonalcoholic beverages

What's not included

Towel

Warm sweater or coverup

Itinerary
Arrive at the manta ray site and receive a briefing from your swim guide.
Enter the water and hold onto a light board that attracts plankton and manta rays.
Float at the surface and watch manta rays feed and glide beneath you for up to 40 minutes.
Return to the boat and cruise back to harbor under the stars.
Highlights

description:

Duration

6 Hours

About

For an out-of-this-world experience, check out Maunakea Stellar Explorer from KapohoKine Adventures. This thrilling adventure is the only Big Island star tour to feature nighttime astronomical observing AND safe daytime solar viewing with all ages welcome! Our in-house stellar explorers are the best and most experienced in Hawaii with one having created the Mauna Kea Visitor’s Center and operated it for a decade. Others on staff have written noted articles and reviews for well-known astronomy magazines and have worked at world-class observatories. The adventure starts with dinner. After dinner your guide will start setting up a state of the art solar telescope for your viewing pleasure, your specially trained guide will let you safely watch the sun come alive in true color with vibrant solar flares, sunspots, prominences and any other current activity weather permitting. Next, you’ll travel across Saddle Road between Mauna Kea and Mauna Loa, two of the tallest and most massive volcanoes on Earth. Your National Park Service trained guide will discuss the volcan

name: Private Sound Bath Meditation Journey
description:
What is a sound bath, you ask?

A sound bath is an immersive sound experience that soothes and aligns your energetic and emotional body and mind into balance! This is done by utilizing harmonic sounds and frequencies generated by crystal bowls and other vibrational instruments. Surrounded by the native ʻōhiʻa lehua forest, you will be guided through a relaxing meditation as a group. All you need to do is show up and your guide will provide everything that you need to feel comfortable and relaxed for this rejuvenating sound experience.

Times and duration
Crystal Bowl and Gong Sound Bath: Wednesdays and Saturdays @ 12:45pm
Crystal Tone Singing Bowl Sound Bath: Sundays and Mondays @ 11am

name: Private Extended Day Rock Climbing
description:
Join our team of professional guides for a guided rock climbing experience like no other. Descend alongside cascading waterfalls in collapsed lava tube canyons surrounded by lush jungle scenery before climbing back out. Closer to the Japanese practice of Sawanobori than the standard rock climbing found in much of the world, this is a unique climbing experience only found in Hawaii.

Due to generally poor rock quality on the island, there is very little climbing available on the Big Island of Hawaii. After 4 years of research, development, and private landowner negotiations, we are excited to finally be able to offer the Island’s first rock climbing experience. This adventure takes you to an area with some of the best rock quality on the island, exclusively accessible to the general public through these tours.

We offer Private extended day trips (6hrs) and half-day trips (3hrs) at a 3-to-1 client-to-guide ratio.

What to Expect

Climb on stunning volcanic rock with panoramic river, waterfall, and rainforest views.

Expert local guides ensure a safe, fun, and personalized adventure.

All gear provided—just bring your sense of adventure!

Half-day and extended-day trips available

Perfect For:

Beginners looking to try outdoor climbing for the first time.

Experienced climbers seeking unique routes and styles of climbing.

name: Private Custom Van Full Day Guided Tour - Hilo, Hawaii
description:
A private van tour is ideal for the group who likes to have a professional Tour Operator be their guide for the day. It allows you the ability to take a tour at your own personal pace and do exactly what you want by customizing a unique experience with your allotted time. Your tour operator can show you popular sites visitors and locals enjoy, as well as the secret places that are not typically included in a large tour.

While we provide several options of possibilities, we can mix and match whatever you want on the fly to accommodate your personalized tour and maximize your day stress-free. Your Professional Tour Operator is familiar with the roads, customs, culture and sites of the Big Island of Hawaii.

Itinerary

When booking a tour, let us know where you’d like to go. We are happy to mix and match to create the perfect tour for you.

Options Hilo Tours:

Water Falls Tour: Akaka Falls, Rainbow Falls, Boiling Pots (3 hours)
Beach Tour: Black Sand Beach Tour and Kalapana Beach (3.5 hours)
Volcano Tour: Volcanoes National Park and Volcano Winery (5 hours)
Coast Tour: Scenic Hamakua coast with Botanical Garden and Waterfalls (3 hours)

name: Hilo Town Haunts
description:
Introducing the "Hilo Town Haunts" Ghost Walk – A Spine-Tingling Journey Through Hilo's Dark History!

Step into the shadowy past of Hilo, Hawaii, with our captivating "Hilo Town Haunts" Ghost Walk. We invite you to join us for an eerie adventure that will send shivers down your spine. As the sun dips below the horizon, we will transport you to a world where restless spirits and chilling tales come to life.

Each location on our "Hilo Town Haunts" Ghost Tour holds a piece of Hilo's enigmatic past, where the veil between the living and the departed is at its thinnest.

Our expert guides are passionate storytellers who will weave the history, mystery, and paranormal tales of each location into a spine-tingling narrative. Whether you are a seasoned ghost hunter or simply curious about the darker side of Hilo, this tour is sure to leave you with a newfound appreciation for the town's enigmatic past.

Expect to learn about Hawaiian's haunted heritage and to hear tales of Hilo's unique paranormal activity. Enigmatic local entities include 'uhane, wandering spirits of the dead, 'aumakua, ancestors transformed into family gods, the protective power of Hawaiian deities such as Madame Pele,, and the akualele, mysterious fireballs associated with curses and the dead.

So, don't miss this opportunity to uncove

Explore the high elevation lava fields of Mauna Loa's northern flank, Earth's largest active volcano

The Experience
We specialize in offering an unforgettable charter fishing experience. Our state-of-the-art 43' Viking Convertible, Lei'n Low, is fully equipped with everything you need for a premier fishing excursion. With twin 800 hp engines and cutting-edge equipment, we get to the best fishing spots quickly and efficiently.

Whether you're an experienced angler or a beginner looking to reel in your first trophy catch, our crew is dedicated to providing an exceptional experience. From the excitement of landing a monster marlin to the relaxation of cruising the beautiful Kona coast.

description:

An introduction to fine chocolate
Our goal during the guided tour and chocolate tasting is to change the way you think about chocolate, from where you purchase it to how you consume it. Hawaii is the only state where cacao is grown commercially, and Honokaa Chocolate is a great place to experience a real cacao tree orchard and to taste world-class fine chocolates. If you’ve never sampled world-class craft chocolate, you’re in for a treat.

Family-Friendly Options
Starting May 13, our tasting bar at the farm will be open by reservation for 45 minute guided chocolate tastings, or you can visit our downtown store between 11am and 4pm every day.

name: Ultimate Circle Island Explorer (Kohala Pick up)
description:
This adventure is designed for the intrepid sightseeing traveler wishing to see as much of the Big Island as possible in one day.
Duration

Approx. 11 Hours

About

This adventure is designed for the intrepid sightseeing traveler wishing to see as much of the Big Island as possible in one day. Travel south into Kona coffee country for the first stop at Bay View Farm, a working 24 acre coffee farm nestled on the slopes of Mauna Loa Volcano just above beautiful Kealakekua Bay and known for the best Kona coffee since 1984. Here you’ll see coffee growing on the trees and learn about the various steps and processes in making coffee, all of which happen right here on the farm. You’ll sample 100% Kona Coffee at a gorgeous overlook with stunning views of Kealakekua Bay and the monument to Captain Cook standing on the only sliver of British land in the United States. The adventure continues as you head through the South Kona and Ka’u Districts. Stunning views, alternating between stark lava flows and Hawaiian dry forest, are visible through the huge picture windows of your tour vehicle. We stop briefly at picturesque Punalu’u Black Sand Beach. Lined with swaying coconut palm trees, this is one

For those looking for a more personalized experience, Aloha Aina Tours offers personalized custom tours. Creating a custom Big Island Tour can be an exciting way to explore the diverse beauty and unique attractions of Hawaii’s largest island. The Big Island is known for its diverse landscapes, from lush rainforests to volcanic deserts, pristine beaches, and towering mountains. Whether you’re interested in exploring specific areas of the island, discovering hidden gems off the beaten path, or crafting an itinerary based on your unique interests, Aloha Aina’s guides will design a tour that fits your preferences.

Why Choose Aloha Aina Tours?
Knowledgeable, local guides with in-depth knowledge of the Big Island’s culture, history, and geology.
Comfortable, small-group tours (14 per van max) that prioritize a personalized and immersive experience.
Opportunities to explore natural wonders, wildlife, and iconic landmarks that make the Big Island one-of-a-kind
We offer custom tour destinations, with various activities and stops, so guests can experience the island at their own pace.

name: South Point Vortex Venture 🌌
description:
Welcome traveler! There is something about Hawaii's healing earth energies that nourish mind, body & soul and encourage spiritual transformation unlike anywhere else in the world.

Journey with us and explore the Big Island's strongest energy vortices as identified in the Hawaii Vortex Field Guide by author Zach Royer.

WHAT TO EXPECT: An elemental experience like no other! Start with an immersion of the grounding energies found at Paleaku Peace Garden (EARTH), a former Buddhist sanctuary turned botanical garden. After tuning ourselves into the vortex, the next stop on the list is the Place of Refuge, a place known for its strong spiritually purifying energies. Then we make our way to South Point (WIND) the southernmost mystery spot in the entire USA followed by a one of a kind 4x4 ride to Green Sands Beach (WATER) which is right nearby. That's only the half of it. Following Green sands, we break for lunch in Naalehu before emanating towards Kilauea volcano and the Volcanoes National Park (FIRE) and the warm embrace of Madame Pele. We end with the volcano so there's a good chance of seeing the glowing lava if conditions are right. Allow these places to encourage healing and bathe in the creative & transformational energies that Hawaii is known for.

Snacks are included and we always stop for lun

name: Elite Volcano Hike-(Kona Pick Up)
description:
Exploring Hawaii Island’s explosive side on foot is what this tour is all about!
Duration

14 Hours

About

Hike Kilauea lava flows dating back to 1881 up to the historic changes of 2018. First, you’ll travel across Saddle Road between Mauna Kea and Mauna Loa, two of the tallest and most massive volcanoes on Earth. Your National Park Service certified guide will discuss the volcanology, geology, and history of these monstrous wonders, as well as providing in-depth narration on the various lava flows and flora and fauna that you’ll be passing by. Next explore Kaumana Caves, part of the lava tube system that brought lava as close as 1.5 miles from downtown Hilo in 1881. Take a peek inside the dramatic cathedral-like space, before taking a quick drive through quaint Hilo town where you’ll drive along the historic waterfront before stopping at KapohoKine Adventures store to pick up supplies. You’re headed to Hawaii Volcanoes National Park where you’ll see the destruction done by the 2018 eruption! After a picnic lunch inside the National Park listening to the native bird calls, you’ll head off along the Halema’uma’u trail to start your longest hike of approximate

name: Elite Volcano Hike- (Hilo Pick Up)
description:
Exploring Hawaii Island’s explosive side on foot is what this tour is all about!
Duration

9 Hours

About

Exploring Hawaii Island’s explosive side on foot is what this tour is all about. Our first stop is Kaumana Caves, part of the lava tube system that brought lava as close as 1.5 miles from downtown Hilo in 1881. Take a peek inside the dramatic cathedral-like space. Next, you’re headed to Hawaii Volcanoes National Park where you’ll see the destruction done by the 2018 eruption! After a picnic lunch inside the National Park listening to the native bird calls, you’ll head off along the Halema’uma’u trail to start your longest hike of approximately three miles. The trail starts behind the historic Volcano House and switches back down to the caldera floor that changed drastically during the 2018 eruption. This trail has been used to hike into the caldera since being established in 1846. Mark Twain was once rescued by native guide Alex Lancaster using this trail. It’s only about half a mile through ancient forests of tree ferns, some as old as 1,000 years. Look for native birds, such as the yellow ‘amakihi and the red ‘apapane. Huge boulders came to rest along

name: Night Manta Snorkel
description:
2.5 Hours • Ages 11+ • 6:30 PM Departure

4.9 
stars
 

447 Google reviews

Kona's #1 Snorkel Tour
Duration

2.5 Hours

About

You’re going to love the way Hawaiian Adventures does mantas! Sharing this incredible experience with our guests is what drives us to run the best manta tour on the Island. Leaving Honokohau Harbor, we have the ability to reach the northern or southern manta sites depending on the calmest conditions. Our 7 PM departure is strategic, as we can choose the site with fewer people and more mantas. With one trip a night, small groups, and professional guides, our tour sets the bar high for value and guest experience.

How to Prepare
Sunscreen

Being sunburnt on your vacation is no fun! While our vessel Artemis has lots of shaded areas, you never know when you’ll be in the sun! The best time to apply sunscreen is either before getting to the marina or before boarding the vessel. Spray-on sunscreen is not allowed onboard.

Shoes Optional!

Artemis was built for comfort! Our vessel features SeaDek, a state-of-the-art closed-cell foam, that is most comfortable when you’re barefoot. Upon boarding our crew will have a bin for shoes, so plan your footwear choice accordingly (sandals recommended).

Motion Sickness

For our whale watching tours, we often head into open water. If you’re prone to motion sickness, please take motion sickness medication the morning of your tour before arriving to the marina.

Snacks

All Hawaiian Adventures Kona tours have complimentary

name: Hilo Haunted Pub Crawl🍹
description:
Duration

2.5 Hours

TOUR OVERVIEW:
The new Hilo Haunted Pub Crawl, offered exclusively by Big Island Ghost Tours, presents a thrilling adventure into the eerie and supernatural side of Hilo, Hawaii. This unique experience invites guests to explore the historic and possibly haunted pubs scattered across the town, under the enigmatic cloak of night. Participants, led by a knowledgeable and theatrically dressed guide, will venture from one establishment to the next, each with its own ghostly tales and mysterious past. The crawl is not just about the spirits found in bottles but also about the stories of the spirits that may still linger in these age-old venues. Equipped with ghost-detecting gadgets, guests will delve into the paranormal, investigating the unseen residents of Hilo's nightlife. The tour blends local lore with the charm of a pub crawl, offering an unforgettable experience of fun, frights, and fascinating stories.

Participants will enjoy the warm hospitality of the local pubs, savoring drinks as they absorb the atmospheric tales and maybe, just maybe, experiencing something supernatural. Beyond the ghost hunting and storytelling, this pub crawl is an opportunity to appreciate the beauty of Hilo at night, from its palm-lined streets to its oceanic breezes, all while mingling with fellow adventurers drawn to the mystery an

name: Kona Ocean Sunset Safari
description:
Embark on our Sunset Dolphin Adventure, where you’ll have the opportunity to encounter Hawaii’s vibrant marine life.

-During the winter months, typically from December to March, the majestic humpback whales migrate to our warm waters for breeding and calving, offering spectacular sightings. Outside of this season, our tours focus on observing playful spinner dolphins, acrobatic bottlenose dolphins, and the occasional sightings of other whale species like pilot and sperm whales, which are present year-round. Each excursion is a unique experience, providing insights into the rich marine ecosystem of the Kona coast.

-Expert Guidance: Our knowledgeable crew will share fascinating facts about whales, their behaviors, and Hawaii's unique marine environment, ensuring a fun and educational experience for all ages.

-Panoramic Ocean Views: Enjoy uninterrupted views of the sparkling Pacific Ocean, frames by Hawaii's stunning coastlines and volcanic backdrops.

-Maring Life Beyond Whales: Alongside whales, keep
highlights: Guided sunset whale watch on boat with United States Coast Guard Licensed Captain and knowledgeable guides.

name: Big Island in a Day Tour from Hilo
description:
See Highlights of the Big Island in a Day from Hilo
Duration

8-9 Hours

About
Experience a diverse range of ecosystems and climate zones of the island
Visit the most dramatic and scenic waterfalls on the island, Akaka and Rainbow Falls,
Learn how the island was formed as you explore active craters, steam vents, and lava tubes of Hawaii Volcanoes National Park
Frequent stops for photos to take in the lush scenery, and spot green sea turtles at Punalu’u Black Sand Beach

This full-day nature tour offers an in-depth look at Hawaii’s youngest and largest island with departures from the City of Hilo. From desert to rainforest, pasture to dry forest, discover diverse landscapes and 8 out of 13 climate zones in the world as you explore the active volcanoes of Hawaii Volcanoes National Park, walk through rainforests to cascading waterfalls, and stroll black-sand beaches as you scan the horizon for green sea turtles.

name: Island of Fire 🔥 Volcano Vortex Tour
description:
Embark on an unforgettable adventure with Big Island Ghost Tours' new Island of Fire Volcano Vortex Day Tour, where you will journey through the volcanic wonders of Hawaii's Big Island. This exclusive driving tour offers an intimate experience, accommodating up to seven passengers, ensuring personalized attention and comfort. Your knowledgeable guide, local author Zach Royer (Hawai'i Vortex Field Guide) will lead you through the majestic & supernatural landscapes shaped by some of the world's most iconic volcanoes, including Hualalai, Kilauea, Mauna Loa, and Mauna Kea, known vortex energy sites. Witness the raw power and beauty of these volcanic giants as you explore their unique terrains, from lush rainforests to stark lava fields, and learn about the rich geological and cultural history that makes this region so extraordinary.

Throughout the tour, you'll have the opportunity to see firsthand the dynamic nature of the island's volcanic activity. Marvel at the stunning sunset vistas from Mauna Kea, the tallest sea mountain in the world, and experience the awe-inspiring sight of Kilauea, one of the most active volcanoes on the planet. The journey also includes stops at lesser-known yet equally fascinating sites, providing a comprehensive look at the island's fiery heart. Whether you're a geology enthu

name: Sound Bath Special Guest - Vocal Sound Meditation led by Odeya Nini (Monday, April 14th)
description:
Join us for a unique Vocal Sound Meditation led by our visiting practitioner and special guest, Odeya Nini!

Located in our beautiful temple space immersed in the vibrant and healing forest sounds of the Kona Cloud Forest Sanctuary.

A total sound immersion in the healing power of the human voice, accompanied by bowls, chimes and gong.

Arrive to surrender to vibration and deeply listen.
Open your heart and mind as you journey through this guided sound experience in meditation to the healing sound waves of dynamic tones and resonant frequency.

Whether you're new or experienced, this gently guided journey supports deep relaxation, energetic balance, and a full-body reset in nature. Get cozy, lay back and receive!

Bio

Odeya Nini is a Los Angeles based interdisciplinary vocalist, composer, embodied vocal coach and sound meditation practitioner. She leads workshops and retreats dedicated to the integration of the mind, body and spirit in releasing the resonant voice and is t

name: Volcano Vortex Explorer Tour🌋
description:
Departure: 9:00 AM sharp | Return: 3:45 PM | Vehicle: Ford Explorer XLT passenger SUV

Step into the heart of Pele’s domain on this one-of-a-kind full-day driving tour to Hawai‘i Volcanoes National Park, specially designed for cruise ship passengers with limited time but unlimited curiosity. Leaving Kona at 9:00 AM, you’ll journey across the Big Island’s dynamic landscape in comfort, guided by a certified Vortex Field Explorer. This isn’t just a scenic tour—it’s a deep dive into the island’s spiritual energy centers, straight from the pages of the Hawai‘i Vortex Field Guide (a copy is included for each guest).

Feel the magnetic pull of the Earth at the park’s most energetic locations, including steam vents, lava tubes, and sacred ground where Hawaiian kahuna once communed with the spirit world. On our return, if time allows, we’ll make a rejuvenating stop at Punaluʻu Black Sand Beach—where sea turtles bask and the mana (spiritual energy) flows strong. With narratives that blend geology, myth, and metaphysics, this tour is a must for seekers, skeptics, and adventurers alike. Be back at the pier well before your ship sets sail, but you’ll carry the experience with you forever.

What's included

🚐 Round-trip transportation in a comfortable passenger SUV

⏰ Timely return to Kailua Pier befor

name: Wildlife Safari
description:
Every trip is custom-tailored to match our guests’ interests and ocean conditions. We typically depart Honokōhau Harbor around 7:00am and head offshore for a pelagic wildlife safari, with nearly two dozen species of dolphins and whales possible to observe, including frequent sightings of pilot whales.

For guests hoping to encounter larger predators, we’ll explore open-ocean sites where oceanic whitetip sharks and whale sharks are sometimes spotted. We typically wrap up the tour with a snorkel at one of Kona’s vibrant coral reefs, exploring lava-formed coastlines full diverse marine life including manta rays.

We will be back at the harbor by 12pm but no sooner than 11am.

What's included

Mask, snorkel, and fins

Assorted snacks

Refreshments

All gear is provided—just bring your sense of adventure!

Itinerary

🚤 Depart Honokōhau Harbor at 7:00am. Return by 12pm.

🌊 Pelagic Wildlife Safari: Head offshore into deep water to search for pilot whales, oceanic whitetips, dolphins, and other big animals

🐠 Coral Reef Snorkel: Explore a vibrant reef along the Kona Coast

🏝️ Scenic Coastal Cruise back to the harbor

Please note: Every trip is different and may vary based on wildlife ac

name: Private Cloud Forest Sanctuary Tour (2.5 hrs)
description:
Immerse yourself in the serene beauty of Hawaiʻi's tropical cloud forest on a peaceful 2.5-hour guided tour of the Kona Cloud Forest Sanctuary - family-forested with love since 1982.

Walk the Extended Montane Jungle Trail, surrounded by stunning rare trees, ferns, palms, bamboo, flowers, and the bird songs of native Hawaiian honeycreepers. Discover the unique climate, geology, and history of this lush ecosystem while learning about its delicate balance and human impact. Breathe fresh mountain air, calm your mind, relax your body, and connect deeply with nature on this unforgettable journey through a mile of sacred trails.

Our mission is to inspire conscious connection with nature, self, and community to cultivate a world where humanity thrives in harmony with the Earth.
This tour is for you if:
You love plants and forests and are ready to become an expert naturalist!
You are eager to explore and learn about the only cloud forest in the United States!
You want the full immersive Kona Cloud Forest Sanctuar

description:
Discover the perfect blend of history, culture, and local charm on the Historic Hawaii and Big Island Market Tour. This half-day adventure takes you on a journey through Hawaii’s rich past and vibrant present, exploring sacred historical sites and immersing yourself in the enchanting energy of a local island market. Along the way, enjoy breathtaking scenery, fascinating stories from our expert guides, and opportunities to sample local flavors and find unique treasures. Whether you’re a history enthusiast or simply looking to explore the Big Island’s heritage, this tour promises unforgettable memories and a deeper appreciation for Hawaii’s rich history.

Perfect for photographers and dolphin and whale enthusiasts

description:
Enjoy a small group guided trail ride, for intermediate and advanced riders, to the Wao Kele o Puna Forest Reserve. Our experienced and knowledgeable guides, and gentle horses, will take you on a relaxing ride on a quiet trail through the lush forest leading up to the Reserve. As you journey, our guides will capture photos of you along the way. The ride ends back at the barn, at our covered picnic area, where you can enjoy a cold beverage, review your amazing photos, and shop for locally made products!

Safety and smart riding are our focus. Helmets are provided free of charge. Riders must be age 13 and up, with a maximum weight of 180 lbs.

description:
Personalized Attention: With smaller group sizes, we ensure that everyone receives personalized attention from our friendly and knowledgeable guides. This allows for a more intimate and engaging experience.

Eco-Friendly Practices: We are dedicated to protecting the marine environment. Our tours follow strict eco-friendly guidelines to minimize our impact on the ocean and its inhabitants, ensuring that future generations can enjoy these majestic creatures.

Expert Guides: Our team consists of marine biologists and experienced naturalists who provide in-depth insights into the behavior and biology of the whales and other marine life. Their passion and expertise make every tour both educational and exciting.

Complimentary Refreshments: Enjoy complimentary refreshments on board, including locally sourced snacks and beverages, to keep you comfortable and refreshed throughout the tour.

Join us for a whale watching experience like no other, witness these magnificent creatures in the wild.
